circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit is a 5. 281 km track located on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi, designed by Hermann Tilke, which has been home to Formula One's season finale since 2009. It represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One venue after Bahrain, with Qatar and Saudi Arabia later joining the calendar.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: Albert Park Circuit is a street track encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, hosting the Formula One Australian Grand Prix each year alongside the Supercars Championship and various supporting events. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: The Red Bull Ring in Spielberg, Austria is a 4. 326 km circuit that has hosted the Austrian Grand Prix during two separate eras. Originally constructed as the Österreichring, it staged the grand prix annually from 1970 to 1987 before undergoing modifications and renaming as the A1-Ring, then serving as the Austrian Grand Prix venue once more from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street circuit in Azerbaijan's capital that measures approximately 6 km, positioning it as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The venue first hosted a grand prix in 2016 as the European Grand Prix, then became the permanent home of the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the 5. 412 km Bahrain International Circuit hosts the region's inaugural Formula One grand prix alongside GP2 racing, drag competitions, and 24-hour endurance events. The venue holds a FIA Grade 1 license and features multiple circuit configurations, though Australian V8 Supercars competed there only briefly between 2006 and 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located near Barcelona in Catalonia, this 4. 657-kilometre Spanish racing facility features a balanced mix of extended straightaways and diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing all aspects of vehicle performance. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 license and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Spa is a 7-kilometre Belgian racing circuit in the Walloon region that has served as the home of the Formula One Belgian Grand Prix since 1925. The track hosted an F1 race in the inaugural 1950 season and has been a regular fixture on the calendar annually since 1985, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km track that serves as the home of the Canadian Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the venue has accommodated various motorsport series including sportscar championships and open-wheel racing competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Located in Shanghai's Jiading District, the Shanghai International Circuit is primarily recognized as the home of the Chinese Grand Prix, which ran annually from 2004 to 2019 before returning in 2024. The venue has expanded its racing calendar to include the Shanghai ePrix beginning in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone Circuit, located in Northamptonshire, England, has been the primary home of the British Grand Prix since 1987, having first held the race in 1948 and subsequently hosting the inaugural World Championship round in 1950. The track alternated with other venues between 1955 and 1986 before becoming the permanent British Grand Prix venue, and continues to host major motorsport events including MotoGP.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, located near Budapest in Mogyoród, hosted the inaugural Formula One Grand Prix held within the Eastern Bloc in 1986, following Bernie Ecclestone's search for a Soviet venue that redirected to Hungary. Built in just eight months starting from late 1985, faster than any previous Formula One circuit's construction, the 4. 381 km track was constructed near a major highway after plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park were rejected by the government.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian circuit constructed in 1922 as the third purpose-built motorsport venue globally and Europe's oldest on the mainland. Home to the Italian Grand Prix continuously since 1949, the track earned its nickname "Temple of Speed" for its extended straights and fast-cornering layout that encourage high-velocity racing.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, situated in Mie Prefecture, Japan, is a 5. 807 km motorsport venue operated by Honda Mobilityland with a spectator capacity of 155,000. The track serves as a prominent host for both Formula One world championship events and the Japanese Super Formula series.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street track that winds through Paradise, Nevada, featuring the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its iconic venues such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas. The layout incorporates the commercial heart of the city, threading between major hotels and casinos that define the region's landscape.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit honoring two Mexican racing brothers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both of whom died in racing accidents during the 1960s and 1970s. The venue has hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix since 2015 on its current layout, having previously staged the race on a different configuration that last appeared in 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile layout with 19 corners and qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Built specifically for the Miami Grand Prix by specialist track designers Apex Circuit Design, this venue joined the Formula One calendar beginning in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street track that winds through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, following the harbour perimeter in the Principality of Monaco. Due to its location predominantly within the Monte Carlo area, the circuit is frequently called by that name both colloquially and in official contexts.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, Circuit Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km track that rejoined Formula One's racing calendar in 2021 hosting the Dutch Grand Prix. The venue's partnership with the championship concludes following the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Situated north of Doha in Qatar, the Lusail International Circuit stretches 5. 419 kilometres and features a main straight extending 1. 068 kilometres.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km Brazilian racing circuit in São Paulo that opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his 1977 death. The track operates in a counterclockwise direction and houses a karting facility dedicated to Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue positioned along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea waterfront, serving as the host for the Kingdom's Formula One Grand Prix since 2021. The track also accommodates international electric racing events starting from 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's downtown waterfront district, creating a temporary racing layout that connects the central business area with the Kallang neighborhood.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km circuit features a balanced mix of extended straightaways and diverse corner types, making it a comprehensive test of driver and vehicle performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 status and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility hosting multiple international racing series, most prominently the Formula One United States Grand Prix. Beyond F1, the venue accommodates NASCAR, MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, while previously staging Australian V8 Supercars, IndyCar, and various sports car championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai-British motorsport competitor who races for the Williams team in Formula One, representing Thailand in the sport.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: The Spanish driver Fernando Alonso represents Aston Martin in Formula One, having secured two world championships during his time with Renault in 2005 and 2006. Over his 23-season career, he has claimed 32 Grand Prix victories and distinguished himself in endurance racing, winning Le Mans twice with Toyota and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to achieve success at both the highest levels of single-seater and sportscar competition.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli joined the Mercedes Formula One team in 2025 and has secured five race victories since entering the championship.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British racing driver currently competing in Formula One with the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Gabi Bortoleto is a Brazilian driver currently racing in Formula One as a member of the Audi team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas currently races for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from a 13-season career that includes 10 Grand Prix victories. He achieved runner-up finishes in consecutive world championships while driving for Mercedes during 2019 and 2020.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto is an Argentine motorsport driver currently racing in Formula One with Alpine.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian driver competing in European Le Mans Series prototypes while holding a reserve role at Haas in Formula One. He made seven Grand Prix appearances during the 2024-2025 season.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The driver achieved a notable career victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while competing for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar is a French-Algerian racing driver who represents France while competing in Formula One with Red Bull Racing.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: British driver Lewis Hamilton competes for Ferrari in Formula One, where he has achieved a remarkable seven world championship titles, matching Michael Schumacher's record. He also holds numerous performance records including the most career victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently competes in Formula One for Audi, bringing significant experience from his extensive career in the sport. Beyond his Formula One endeavors, he achieved notable success in endurance racing, including a victory at the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson, a New Zealand motorsport competitor, currently drives for Racing Bulls in Formula One.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc represents Monaco in Formula One as a Ferrari driver, having secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his nine-season career. He finished second in the 2022 drivers' championship while competing for the Scuderia.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship with WRT as a BMW factory driver, while also participating part-time in NASCAR's Cup Series. His Formula One career spanned two periods, from 2014 to 2020 and again from 2022 to 2024.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver, claimed the 2025 Formula One championship while racing for McLaren, adding to his tally of 11 Grand Prix victories accumulated over eight seasons in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix victory he achieved with Alpine remains a significant highlight of his career.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Sergio "Checo" Pérez is a Mexican driver currently racing in Formula One for Cadillac. The accomplished competitor finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while with Red Bull and has claimed six race victories throughout his 15-year career in the sport.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver, currently races for McLaren in Formula One, where he has secured nine Grand Prix victories over his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One over 14 seasons from 2011 until 2024, during which he secured eight Grand Prix victories before retiring from the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell, a British driver, has secured seven Grand Prix victories during his eight-season tenure with Mercedes in Formula One.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has secured four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: American driver Logan Sargeant competed in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to endurance racing. He currently races in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, having previously driven LMP2 machinery for Era Motorsport in IMSA competition.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin, competing at the sport's highest level.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Japanese driver Yuki Tsunoda participated in Formula One racing from 2021 through 2025, currently holding a reserve driver position with Red Bull Racing and its sister team Racing Bulls.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen has dominated Formula One racing for Red Bull Racing, claiming four consecutive world championships between 2021 and 2024. With 71 race victories accumulated over 12 seasons, he stands as one of the sport's most successful competitors under the Dutch flag.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is a Chinese racing driver currently holding a reserve position with Cadillac in Formula One, making him the sole driver from China to have competed in the sport. He participated in the championship between 2022 and 2024 before transitioning to his present reserve role.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, representing Renault's sports car brand following the rebranding of the previous Renault F1 Team. Operating with a French licence and powered by Mercedes engines, Alpine secured its only victory during this era when Esteban Ocon won the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, before Renault's withdrawal from the sport concluded the team's tenure as a manufacturer-backed operation at the end of 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has entered Formula One multiple times throughout the sport's history, with its current iteration operating as AMR GP Limited and competing under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's longest-established and most accomplished team, having participated in every world championship since the sport's inception in 1950. The squad, nicknamed after their iconic rearing horse emblem, maintains an unbroken presence in the championship that no other competitor can claim.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas established this American-licensed Formula One team in 2014, debuting on the grid in 2016. Ayao Komatsu assumed leadership as team principal in 2024, taking over from Guenther Steiner, who had held the position since the team's formation.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Peter Sauber's Swiss engineering outfit, established in 1970, became a Formula One constructor in 1993 and competed for over three decades as one of motorsport's longest-running independent teams. The squad secured notable endurance racing success, including the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours victory, before concluding its F1 operations in 2025 as the sport's fourth-most experienced constructor by race entries. Audi's acquisition of the team and infrastructure in 2024 set the foundation for the German manufacturer's future F1 programme.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing Limited is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at the McLaren Technology Centre in Woking, Surrey, operating as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group under the ownership of Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has maintained a presence in Formula One since 1954 through both team ownership and engine supply. The current Mercedes-AMG Petronas team, operating from Brackley under a German licence, saw Ineos join as equal co-owner alongside Mercedes-Benz Group and Toto Wolff in 2022. The marque's racing efforts have earned the "Silver Arrows" designation among fans and media.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced operations in 2024 as one of two Red Bull-owned entities competing in the series. Based in Faenza with an additional facility near its sister team in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as a second outfit for the Austrian conglomerate's motorsport division.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that entered the sport in 2024, operating as one of two teams under Red Bull's ownership alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with a secondary facility near Milton Keynes, the team represents Red Bull's expansion within the grid through a second competitive entry.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Red Bull Racing is an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and owned by Red Bull GmbH, which also operates Racing Bulls as its second team in the sport. Founded in 2005, the team was led by Christian Horner until his departure in 2025, when Laurent Mekies assumed the role of team principal.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams is a British Formula One constructor founded by Frank Williams and Patrick Head in 1977, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes as Atlassian Williams F1 Team in 2026 and remains one of the sport's established operations.
